title: 第 84 節
en: 7. This Mahaduktha is the highest development 1 VaX>, speech, taking the form of Anush/ubh, and being joined with the Rik, or the Brz'hati, touches the true, i. e. Prawa, breath, which is to be meditated on under the form of the Brz'hati. Comm.
2 Cf. Ait. Ar. II, 2, 3, 4.
3 Because the Anush/ubh is made out of the Bn'hatf, the Bnhati being breath, therefore the Anush/ubh is called its body.
of speech, and it is fivefold, viz. measured, not measured, music, true, and untrue.

8. A Rik verse, a gatha 1, a kumbya - are measured (metrical). A Ya^us line, an invocation, and general remarks 3, these are not measured (they are in prose). A Saman, or any portion (parvan) of it, is music. Om is true, Na is untrue.
9. What is true (Om) is the flower and fruit of speech. He is able to become celebrated and of good report, for he speaks the true (Om), the flower and fruit of speech.

10. Now the untrue is the root 4 of speech, and as a tree whose root is exposed dries up and perishes, thus a man who says what is untrue exposes his root, dries up and perishes. Therefore one should not say what is untrue, but guard oneself from it.

11. That syllable Om (yes) goes forward (to the first cause of the world) and is empty. Therefore if a man says Om (yes) to everything, then that (which he gives away) is wanting to him here 5. If he says Om (yes) to everything, then he would empty himself, and would not be capable of any enjoyments.
12. That syllable Na (no) is full for oneself6. If a man says No to everything, then his reputation 1 A gatha is likewise in verse, for instance, prata/2 pratar anrztara te vadanti.
2 A kumbya is a metrical precept, such as, brahmaMryasyaposanam karma kuru, diva ma svapsi/^, &c.
5 Such as arthavadas, explanatory passages, also gossip, such as is common in the king's palace, laughing at people, &c.
4 As diametrically opposed to the flowers and fruits which represent the true. Comm.
Then that man is left empty here on earth for that enjoyment. Comm.
6 He who always says No, keeps everything to himself.
II ARAiVYAKA, 3 ADHYAYA, 7 KHA2VDA, I. 23 I would become evil, and that would ruin him even here.
13. Therefore let a man give at the proper time only, not at the wrong time. Thus he unites the true and the untrue, and from the union of those two he grows, and becomes greater and greater.

14. He who knows this speech of which this (the mahaduktha) is a development, he is clever. A is the whole of speech, and manifested through different kinds of contact (mutes) and of wind (sibilants), it becomes manifold and different.

15. Speech if uttered in a whisper is breath, if spoken aloud, it is body. Therefore (if whispered) it is almost hidden, for what is incorporeal is almost hidden, and breath is incorporeal. But if spoken aloud, it is body, and therefore it is perceptible, for body is perceptible.
Seventh Kha-yda.

1. This (nishkevalya-^astra) becomes perfect as a thousand of Br/hatis. It is glory (the glorious Brahman, not the absolute Brahman), it is Indra. Indra is the lord of all beings. He who thus knows Indra as the lord of all beings, departs from this world by loosening the bonds of life l — so said Mahidasa Aitareya. Having departed he becomes Indra (or Hira^yagarbha) and shines in those worlds2.

1 The commentator explains visrasa by 'merging his manhood in the identity with all,' and doing this while still alive. Visras is the gradual loosening of the body, the decay of old age, but here it has the meaning of vairagya rather, the shaking off of all that ties the Self to this body or this life.
2 The fourteen worlds in the egg of Brahman. Comm. Some hold that he who enters on this path, and becomes deity, does not 232 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.

2. And with regard to this they say: 'If a man obtains the other world in this form (by meditating on the pra/^a, breath, which is the uktha, the hymn of the mahavrata), then in what form does he obtain this world J ? '

3. Here the blood of the woman is a form of Agni (fire) ; therefore no one should despise it. And the seed of the man is a form of Aditya (sun) ; therefore no one should despise it. This self (the woman) gives her self (skin, blood, and flesh) to that self (fat, bone, and marrow), and that self (man) gives his self (fat, bone, and marrow) to this self (skin, blood, and flesh). Thus 2 these two grow together. In this form (belonging to the woman and to fire) he goes to that world (belonging to the man and the sun), and in that form (belonging to man and the sun) he goes to this world (belonging to the woman and to fire 3).
Eighth Khanda.
1. Here (with regard to obtaining Hira^yagarbha) there are these .51okas :

arrive at final liberation. Others, however, show that this identification with the uktha, and through it with the pra«a (breath) and Hirawyagarbha, is provisional only, and intended to prepare the mind of the worshipper for the reception of the highest knowledge of Brahman.
1 The last line on page 246 should, I think, be the penultimate line of page 247.

2 The body consists of six elements, and is hence called sha/kau-rika. Of these, three having a white appearance (fat, bone, and marrow), come from the sun and from man ; three having a red appearance, come from fire and from the woman.
3 It is well therefore to shake off this body, and by meditating on the uktha to obtain identity with Hira«yagarbha. Coram.
zh: 這個摩訶度刻塔（Mahaduktha）是最高層次的發展¹梵文，言語採取阿奴施婆（Anushubh）的形式，與梨克（Rik）相結合，或與布瑞哈蒂（Brihati）相結合，觸及真實的、即普拉瓦（Prawa）、呼吸，應當在布瑞哈蒂的形式下進行觀想。——注釋
2 參見《愛德勒亞蘭亞卡》（Ait. Ar.）II，2, 3, 4。
因為阿奴什圖巴（Anushṭubh）是由勃里哈蒂（Bṛhati）構成的，勃里哈蒂乃是呼吸，因此阿奴什圖巴被稱為它的身體。
言語之事，為五重，即：有度量者、無度量者、音樂、真實、與虛妄。
8. 梨克詩句（Rik verse）、迦陀（gatha）、昆比亞（kumbya）是有韻律的。雅柔斯經句（Ya^us line）、祈禱文及一般性言論，這些並非有韻律的（它們是散文體）。沙摩讚（Saman）或其任何部分（parvan）乃是音樂。「唵」（Om）為真，「那」（Na）為不真。
9. 真實之物（Om）乃言語的花果。他能夠聲名遠播、享有美譽，蓋因他言說真實之物（Om），即言語的花果。
10. 蓋不真實乃言說之根 4，如樹其根既露則枯萎而亡，故言不真實之人亦暴露其根、枯萎而亡。是以人應勿言不真實，當自守而遠離之。
11. 那個音節「唵」（是）向前走去（趨向世界的第一因），因此是空虛的。所以如果一個人對一切都說「唵」（是），那麼（他所施予的）在此世對他便有所欠缺。如果他對一切都說「唵」（是），那麼他將使自己空虛，並且無法享受任何福樂。
12. 那個音節「那」（na，意為「非」）對自己來說是充分的。6 如果一個人對一切都說「非」，那麼他的聲譽 註：原文此處似有缺漏或殘缺，因為句子在「他的聲譽」後未見完整。 1 蓋陀（gatha）同樣是詩歌形式，例如 prata/2 pratar anrztara te vadanti。
甘巴（kumbya）是一種韻律格言，例如：brahmaMryasyaposanam karma kuru、diva ma svapsi 等等。
例如解釋性段落（arthavadas）、以及宮廷中常見的閒談，譬如嘲笑他人等。
4 與代表真理的花卉和果實完全相反。註。
那麼這個人就在地上被留下來，空無一物，無法享受。注釋。
凡事皆曰「不」者，乃自守其所有。
II 阿蘭耶卡（Araṇyaka），第3阿地耶亞（Adhyaya），第7卡翁達（Khaṇḍa），I. 23 我會變成邪惡的，那樣即使在此世也會毀滅他。
13. 因此，人應當在恰當的時刻施予，而非在不恰當的時刻。如是他便將真與非真相結合，由此二者的結合中，他得以增長，並日益昌盛。
14. 誰知曉此言辭——摩訶度克沙（mahaduktha）乃其展開者——彼人聰慧。甲（A）乃言辭之全體，透過各種接觸音（mutes）與氣流音（sibilants）之不同形式得以顯現，因而成為繁多與殊異。
15. 言語如果以低聲細語道出，便是呼吸；如果大聲說出，便是身體。因此（若低聲細語），它幾乎是隱沒的，因為無形之物幾乎是隱沒的，而呼吸是無形的。但若大聲說出，它便是身體，因此它是可感知的，因為身體是可感知的。
第七《迦耶陀》卷。
這（nishkevalya-śāstra）以千個婆利提（Brihati）而臻於完美。它是榮光（榮光的梵天，非絕對的梵天），它是因陀羅。因陀羅是一切眾生的主宰。誰如此認知因陀羅為一切眾生的主宰，就會鬆解生命之繫縛而離開此世——馬希達薩‧艾達雷亞（Mahidasa Aitareya）如是說。既已離去，他成為因陀羅（或希蘭亞迦爾巴），並在那些世界中閃耀。
評註家將 visrasa 解釋為「將其人性融合於與萬物的同一性中」，且此舉發生於生前。Visras 本義為身體逐漸鬆弛、老年衰退，但此處則具有 vairagya 的含義，即震盪脫落束縛自我於此身或此生的一切。
2 梵天之卵中的十四個世界。注釋：有些人認為，那些踏上此道並成為提婆者，不會 232 愛達雷亞奧義書。
2. 而就此事他們說：「若一人以此種形式獲得彼世（藉由靜慮於 pra/^a、呼吸，即 uktha、大祭讚歌），那麼他以何種形式獲得此世呢？」

3. 此處女人的血液是阿格尼（火）的一種形式；因此，沒有人應該輕視它。而男人的精液是阿迪提亞（太陽）的一種形式；因此，沒有人應該輕視它。這個自我（女人）將她的自我（皮膚、血液和肉體）給予那個自我（脂肪、骨骼和骨髓），而那個自我（男人）將他的自我（脂肪、骨骼和骨髓）給予這個自我（皮膚、血液和肉體）。如此這兩者一起成長。他以這種形式（屬於女人和火）進入那個世界（屬於男人和太陽），並以那種形式（屬於男人和太陽）進入這個世界（屬於女人和火）。
第八章
這裡（關於獲得希拉尼亞葛巴（Hiranyagarbha））有這些頌詩（ślokas）：
抵達最終的解脫。然而，其他經文則表明，與讚頌詞（uktha）的這種同一性，以及透過它與生命氣（pra«a）及金胎（Hirawyagarbha）的同一性，僅是過渡性的，其目的在於為信徒的心靈做好準備，使其能夠接受關於梵（Brahman）的最高知識。
我認為第246頁最後一行應該是第247頁倒數第二行。
身體由六種元素組成，因此稱為 śakārika。其中三種具有白色外觀（脂肪、骨骼和骨髓），來自太陽和男性；三種具有紅色外觀，來自火和女性。
因此最好是拋卻此身，透過冥想烏克塔（uktha）而獲得與希蘭亞迦爾巴（Hirayagarbha）的同一性。考霍恩本。
